page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor operating at 2.5 GHz, configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The hardware allocation provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves average tier results in single-core operations, as demonstrated by its stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including Passmark CPU and multi-core Geekbench tests, fall into the lower performance tiers. Memory read and write speeds are relatively constrained, which impacts data-intensive operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high memory capacity relative to its processing cores, making it a viable option for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint without demanding intensive parallel processing. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this server is best suited for lightweight database hosting, basic web serving, memory-cached applications, and general-purpose tasks that do not require high multi-threaded compute performance or local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz, delivering 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established at a baseline of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose applications that benefit from a larger memory footprint without requiring high multi-threaded processing power.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight database hosting and memory-cached applications.
